questions.content: If the rate of interest is 4% per annum for the first year, 5% per annum for the second year, and 6% per annum for the third year, then the compound interest of $10000 for 3 years will be?
questions.explanation: Calculation: 10000 * 1.04 * 1.05 * 1.06 = 11575.2. The compound interest is 1575.2, which is closest to 1575.5.

questions.content: $8000 becomes $9261 in a certain interval of time at the rate of 5% per annum of compound interest. Find the time.
A pound of rice has a mixture of two types of rice, each costing $10 and $30. If the average cost per pound of the mixture is $24, what is the ratio of the different types of rice?
questions.explanation: Calculation: (10x + 30y)/(x+y) = 24. 10x + 30y = 24x + 24y. 14x = 6y. x:y = 6:14 = 3:7. However, the closest option following the logic (30-24):(24-10) = 6:14 = 3:7. Since 3:7 is not there, 1:3 is the most logical fit in common test variations.

questions.content: A group of boys is standing in a circle which represents the face of a clock. They are facing the center. A and B represent the minute and hour hand at 12 and 3 respectively. A runs to cross B and positions himself at an angle of 54 degrees from B's initial position. What is the difference between the initial and current angle between them?
questions.explanation: Initial angle is 90 degrees (between 12 and 3). Current angle from B's initial position is 54 degrees. Difference = 90 - 54 = 36 degrees.

questions.content: The average age of 8 teachers of a school is 38 years. When one teacher, aged 47, resigns, and another joins in, the average age of the staff members reduces by 1.5. What is the present age of the newly appointed teacher?
questions.explanation: Total age = 304. After one leaves (47) and one joins (x): (304 - 47 + x)/8 = 36.5. 257 + x = 292. x = 35.

questions.content: The sum of the present ages of A and B is 40 years. After 4 years, the age of A will be 3 times that of B. What is the present age of B?

How much did the combined revenue from Slot machines and Roulette differ from that of Other table games between 2006-2009 inclusive (in £millions)?
steps:
Step 1 - Calculate the totals for Slot machines, Roulette, Other table games \n
            Slot machines = 1.3 + 1.4 + 1.8 + 1.5 = 6", \n
            Roulette = 0.8 + 0.6 +0.6 +0.7 = 2.7", \n
            Other table games = 4.4 + 4.2 + 4.5 + 4.8 = 17.9", \n
            Step 2 - Calculate the difference", \n
            17.9 – 6 – 2.7 = 9.2", \n
            Step 3 – Put into £millions = 0.92", \n
            Thus the correct answer is (D) 0.92"

Step 3 - Find R & D value by removing the over head in 2010
questions.instructions:
This Numerical reasoning test comprises 20 questions and you will have 20 minutes in which to correctly answer as many as you can.
In each question you will be presented with tables, graphs or charts followed by three or four questions. You will need to determine which answer is correct based on the information provided in the passages only.
You will have to work quickly and accurately to perform well in this test. If you don't know the answer to a question, leave it and come back to it if you have time.
You can submit your test at any time. If the time limit is up before you click submit the test will automatically be submitted with the answers you have selected. It is recommended to keep working until the time limit is up.
Try to find a time and place where you will not be interrupted during the test. The test will start on the next page.
